narration: The average tax refund is $3,011. It hits your account and feels like free money — but here's the thing: it's actually a trap.
visual: Phone falling into frame showing a +$3,011 direct deposit notification from IRS. Animated dollar counter spinning up to $3,011. Text overlay: 'FEELS LIKE FREE MONEY'.

narration: That refund isn't a gift — it's an interest-free loan you gave the IRS. They held your money all year and paid you back zero percent.
visual: Grid of 12 month boxes (JAN-DEC) on light background, each showing -$251 flowing toward the IRS with an arrow. Small return arrow showing $3,011 back. Red banner: 'AT 0% INTEREST'.

narration: And 65% of people spend their full refund within 30 days. Number one: electronics. Number two: vacations. Number three: random impulse buys that change nothing.
visual: Dark background with three items springing in: TV (electronics #1), airplane (vacations #2), shopping bag (impulse #3). Progress bar filling up labeled '30-DAY WINDOW'.

narration: But if you invested that same $3,000 at 8% returns, in 30 years you'd have $30,187. One decision, three decades of difference.
visual: Light background. Growing bar chart showing $3,000 compounding over 7 milestones yr0 to yr30. Final bar glows amber. Counter animates to $30,187. Label: '+907% GROWTH'.

narration: The fix is simple — adjust your W-4 so you take home $250 more each month. Invest it automatically as it comes in, instead of waiting for a lump sum.
visual: Dark background. W-4 form slides in from the left. Twelve monthly green bars build up one by one representing $250/month. Green banner: 'INVEST MONTHLY'.

narration: Your refund isn't a bonus — it's your own money holding you back. Comment 'REFUND' and I'll show you exactly how to fix your W-4 right now.
visual: Dark background. Piggy bank springs in with gold coins falling into the slot. Bold text: 'HOLDING YOU BACK.' Amber CTA button: 'COMMENT REFUND'.
